concerns.text: FR-005 mandates n=500 permutations. Task T026 implements n=100. No task exists to implement the 500-permutation requirement or to document the deviation as a plan change. The spec requirement is unimplemented.

FR-002 mandates 'motion correction' and 'normalization'. Task T014 description explicitly suggests 'simulating if raw preprocessing is too heavy' or loading preprocessed data, effectively skipping the mandatory preprocessing steps. No task exists to implement the actual motion correction/normalization pipeline.

FR-012 requires sensitivity analysis on the 'cognitive decline threshold definition (±1 point variation)'. Task T027 only mentions sweeping 'decision thresholds' (0.45/0.50/0.55). No task exists to re-calculate labels based on the ±1 point score variation and re-run the classification.

FR-011 requires validation against external clinical outcomes (MCI) and documentation if unavailable. Task T022 checks for data and flags it, but there is no explicit task to 'document this limitation in the final report' as part of the report generation flow (T028), leaving the requirement partially unimplemented.

Task T004 references dataset 'ds000248'. Constitution VI and Spec US-1 mandate 'ds000246'. This is a stale tag referencing a non-existent (for this project) dataset ID, creating a traceability mismatch.
Task T026 references dataset 'ds000248' (implied by context of T004/T013). Constitution VI mandates 'ds000246'. Stale tag referencing the wrong dataset ID.
